summary: Full backend + frontend testing of nutrition (БЖУ) import from .docx. Parser correctly handles per-dish tables (real file: 67 records) and flat tables. Matcher correctly identifies matched/ambiguous/unmatched. Endpoint edge cases (non-.docx, empty, no tables) return 400. Apply flow persists nutrition_* fields. Frontend NutritionImportDialog shows preview with correct counts (46 matched / 17 ambiguous / 4 unmatched), counter decreases on uncheck.
critical_code_review_comments: Parser edge quirk (not user-facing today): the 'per-dish table' branch triggers whenever a table has 3..5 rows. If a user ever supplies a flat header+data table with only 2-4 data rows (total 3-5), parser would treat it as per-dish and lose data. Real user file uses per-dish format so not affected. Consider tightening the heuristic (e.g. only take per-dish branch when row0 spans <=1 column with text and last row has >=5 numeric cells).
nutrition_import endpoint uses HTTPException(400, msg) positional signature — works but non-standard; prefer HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=msg) for consistency.
In apply loop, when apply_ids is provided, item_id belonging to matched but excluded is silently skipped. No feedback in response about skipped items — could be useful ('skipped: N').
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ prefixed dishes and clean them up via delete in finally blocks.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Nutrition import feature verified end-to-end. Real user file /tmp/bzu.docx contains 67 records → 46 matched / 17 ambiguous / 4 unmatched against admin's live restaurant menu. All new tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_nutrition_import.py and cover parser (per-dish + flat + real file), matcher (exact + ambiguous + unmatched), endpoint edge cases (400 for non-.docx / empty / no-tables), dry-run preview shape, apply persistence with apply_ids filter. Frontend UI dialog verified: correctly opens from admin/menu → shows 3 sections (matched with checkboxes, ambiguous chips, unmatched list) + live counter + apply button.
